Title: Hibernian 1-2 Heart of Midlothian | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:
Blair Spittal was the Edinburgh Derby hero for Heart of Midlothian against nine-man Hibernian at Easter Road. Martin Boyle fired the hosts into the lead on his final derby appearance for Hibs but goalkeeper Raphael Sallinger soon saw red for handling the ball outside the box. Hibernian were under further pressure early in the second half when Felix Passlack recieved a second yellow for a high challenge bringing the home side down to nine men. Lawrence Shankland forced the ball home to bring Hearts level and it was Spittal who netted the winner minutes from time to ensure Derek McInnes' men maintained their three points lead at the top of the William Hill Premiership with four games to go.

Title: Heart of Midlothian 2-1 Rangers | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:

Title: Hearts 1-1 Kilmarnock | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:
A last minute Bruce Anderson equaliser earned Kilmarnock a point at Tynecastle and consigned Hearts to a fourth straight game without a win.  A cute Lawrence Shankland finish just after half-time looked to have secured the 3 points for Derel McInnes’ side, but they were denied right at the death when Alexander Schwolow was unable to deal with a long range Liam Polworth strike and Anderson tucked away the rebound to the delight of the travelling Killie support. Hearts remain top of the William Hill Premiership, but now only on goal difference from champions Celtic who have a game in hand.

Title: Heart of Midlothian 1-1 Dundee United | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:
Hearts dropped points at Tynecastle for the first time since August. Yevhenii Kucherenko spilled Alexandros Kyziridis’s effort into his own net and the league leaders went in front. Luca Stephenson pulled one back with a great header from a corner to equalise for the visitors. Zac Sapsford and Amar Fateh had goals ruled out by the assistant referee, and United had to settle for a point.

Title: Heart of Midlothian 4-0 Dundee | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:
With Celtic in Premier Sports Cup action, Hearts took their chance to extend their lead at the top of the William Hill Premiership to nine points. Lawrence Shankland fired a terrific shot past Jon McCracken to take the lead at Tynecastle before Pierre Landry Kaboré doubled the home side’s lead, taking advantage after some confusion from a corner kick. A terrific effort from Blair Spittal was tipped onto the bar by McCracken but Kaboré was quick to the rebound to extend Hearts’ lead. Tomas Magnusson rounded off proceedings with his first goal in maroon and the Jambos’ title charge continues.

Title: Heart of Midlothian 3-1 Celtic | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:

Title: Rangers 0-2 Heart of Midlothian | HIGHLIGHTS | William Hill Premiership
Description:
Heart of Midlothian made a triumphant return to the top of the William Hill Premiership and put themselves nine points clear of Rangers after just five matches.. Lawrence Shankland was heavily linked with a move to Ibrox last season and he was at the double for Derek McInnes side with a tidy finish in the first half and converting a penalty in the second. Russell Martin will be looking for answers as his side have yet to win in the league.
